news.headline: Spain will play Germany in Euro 2024 Quarterfinals after defeating Georgia 4-1.
news.summary: In the latest report concerning the economic situation, it has been revealed that the national unemployment rate has decreased to 4.3%, the lowest it has been in the last decade. This signifies a substantial improvement compared to the previous year when the unemployment rate was recorded at 6.2%. Additionally, approximately 250,000 new jobs were created in various sectors of the economy, showing a significant growth in employment opportunities. The report also mentions that the GDP has experienced a growth of 5.2% in the last quarter, indicating a strong and thriving economy. Moreover, inflation remains stable at 2.1%, providing a favorable environment for both businesses and consumers. The government officials have expressed optimism regarding these statistics and are aiming to sustain this positive trend in the coming years.

news.headline: MOGAS wins multimillion $ contract for South Korean S-Oil Shaheen Project to commercialize TC2C™ technology.
news.summary: Houston-based company MOGAS Industries has secured a lucrative multimillion-dollar contract for the S-Oil Shaheen Project in South Korea. This deal marks the debut commercial use of TC2C™ (Thermal Crude to Chemicals) technology. Developed by Lummus Technology, TC2C™ enables the conversion of whole crude and refinery orphan streams into valuable olefin and aromatic products, including operating one of the globe’s largest steam crackers. This achievement builds on prior accomplishments in projects involving ebullated bed hydrocracking.

news.headline: Avoid trading tokens with fake volumes on crypto exchanges by leveraging data with these 3 strategies.
news.summary: There has been a significant rise in manipulated trading volumes on certain cryptocurrency exchanges. To protect yourself from falling victim to this, consider utilizing data analysis in three key ways. By scrutinizing trading patterns and volumes, conducting thorough research on exchange credibility and performance, and comparing data across multiple platforms, investors can avoid being misled by false trading indicators. This proactive approach can help safeguard against fraudulent activities and ensure a more secure trading experience in the volatile cryptocurrency market.

news.headline: South Korea's 2nd biggest chipmaker plans $75 billion AI investment by 2028, following $90B for chip facility.
news.summary: Following a pledge made in March to invest $90 billion in a semiconductor fabrication plant located near Seoul, additional funds have been allocated to support the project.

news.headline: DOJ reportedly offers Boeing a plea deal over 737 Max crashes, families of victims not satisfied with terms.
news.summary: An attorney representing the families affected by the crashes in 2018 and 2019 has stated that the proposed agreement does not meet the families' expectations in terms of compensation and justice. The families had originally sought a higher settlement amount and further accountability for the incidents.

news.headline: Apple is revisiting strategies from the 1980s to enhance AI technology.

Lindsay Corp beats Q3 earnings estimate with $1.85 EPS, but revenue dips 15.4% Y/Y to $139 million. Backlog grows to $246.9 million compared to $94.5 million last year. Operating income drops to $20 million from $27 million. Portfolio adjustments suggested.
news.summary: Lindsay Corporation (NYSE: LNN) reported adjusted earnings per share of $1.41 in Q3 fiscal 2024, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.25 but witnessing an 8% drop from the previous year. When factoring in an income tax credit of 44 cents per share, earnings per share stood at $1.85. Revenues for the quarter were $139 million, down 15.4% from the previous year and below the estimated $152 million. The company's backlog as of May 31, 2024, rose to $246.9 million from $94.5 million in 2023. Operating revenue costs decreased by 16.7% to $93 million, leading to a gross profit decline of 12.8% to $46.5 million, with a gross margin of 33.4%. Operating expenses were $26.6 million, up 1.1% year over year, resulting in an operating income of $20 million, a decrease from the previous year. The Irrigation segment's revenues dropped by 19% to $115 million, with North America irrigation revenues decreasing by 9% to $68 million. These figures indicate a challenging quarter for Lindsay Corporation.

news.headline: Galaxy Research predicts minor market impact from Mt. Gox Bitcoin distributions of 64,000 out of 142,000 BTC, many may not be immediately sold. They are also optimistic about Ethereum ETF inflows.
news.summary: In a recent podcast, Galaxy Research's Alex Thorn discussed the expected market impact of the upcoming Mt. Gox Bitcoin (BTC) distributions. Thorn predicts that out of the total 142,000 BTC, only around 64,000 BTC will be distributed to individual creditors. He believes that a large portion of this amount is unlikely to be immediately sold. Thorn emphasizes that the selling of Gox Bitcoin may not significantly impact the market, as many creditors are long-term holders. He suggests that even if 10% of the 64,000 BTC is sold (equivalent to 6,400 BTC), the overall impact on the market may be minimal. Thorn remains cautiously optimistic about the inflows to Ethereum (ETH) ETFs.

news.headline: Apogee's Q1 earnings beat estimates with $1.44 EPS, revenue at $331.5M (down 8.3% YoY). Gross margin reached 29.8%.
news.summary: Apogee Enterprises, Inc. (NASDAQ: APOG) reported first-quarter fiscal 2025 earnings per share of $1.44, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.16, marking a 37.1% increase year over year. Despite beating expectations, the company saw revenues of $331.5 million, down 8.3% from the previous year due to lower volume. Cost of sales fell 13.4% to $233 million, with a gross profit increase of 6.3% to $99 million and a gross margin expansion to 29.8%. Operating income rose 22.5% to $41 million, driven by a 2.9% decrease in selling, general, and administrative expenses to $57 million. In the Architectural Framing Systems segment, revenues fell 18.9% to $133 million, leading to an operating profit of $18.4 million. The impact of lower volumes was partially offset by favorable material costs, mix, and productivity gains.

news.headline: SEC sues Consensys for unregistered brokerage activities. Allegations involve 36 million crypto transactions, 5 million in securities.
news.summary: The U.S. SEC has filed a lawsuit against Consensys, claiming violations of securities laws for not registering as a broker and not registering securities offerings. The complaint focuses on Consensys' services, MetaMask Swaps and MetaMask Staking, facilitating over 36 million crypto asset transactions, including 5 million involving crypto asset securities without proper registration. MetaMask Swaps allowed users to trade crypto assets with the best exchange rates from 14 third-party liquidity providers, all handled through Consensys' smart contracts for seamless transfers. The SEC alleges significant concerns for investor protection in this case.

news.headline: Kellanova (NYSE: K) upgraded to Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) due to rising earnings estimates. This may lead to increased buying pressure and stock price growth.
news.summary: Kellanova (NYSE: K) has been recently upgraded to a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), indicating a positive outlook for its earnings estimates. This upgrade, based on changing EPS estimates from analysts, highlights the potential increase in stock prices. The Zacks rating system focuses on objective factors like earnings picture, in contrast to subjective analyst upgrades. This upgrade for Kellanova suggests a positive earnings outlook which could lead to a stock price increase. Earnings estimate revisions have proven to be a significant factor impacting stock prices. Investors can consider Kellanova as a solid addition to their portfolio given this optimistic forecast.

news.headline: Calls for Democratic Party to replace Biden due to age concerns post first 2024 debate. Potential replacements trending include Newsom, Clinton, and Obama. Biden plans to not drop out. 25th Amendment trending on social media. Democratic National Committee convention starts August 19.
news.summary: The first 2024 presidential debate between Joe Biden and Donald Trump was held, where Trump was named the winner in a poll by CNN. Biden struggled in the debate, leading to calls for the Democratic Party to replace him due to concerns over his age. Possible replacements mentioned include Hillary Clinton, Michelle Obama, and Gavin Newsom. The 25th Amendment and the question of who could replace Biden as the Democratic candidate in the upcoming election are trending on social media. Biden has already won pledged delegates for the 2024 election, but there is speculation about his potential withdrawal, which his campaign denies. The Democratic National Committee convention is set to start on August 19. The article can be found on Benzinga.com for further details.

Overall, Lennox International's recent upgrade to a Zacks Rank #2 highlights its potential as a strong addition to investment portfolios.

news.headline: SOL's ETF potential spurs price surge 8.9x as ADA, XRP miss out. BTC activity at decade low. Steve Cohen closes crypto fund. Kraken donates $1m to Trump. Bolivia lifts BTC ban; MegaLabs secures $20m for 100k TPS.
news.summary: SOL is leading the major cryptocurrencies, with Van Eck filing for an ETF. Bitwise has suggested that a SOL ETF filing may be on the horizon, while ADA and XRP are not expected to receive ETF approval, according to GSR. GSR predicts that the price of SOL could potentially increase by 8.9x with the introduction of an ETF. Coinbase and Stripe have collaborated to facilitate onboarding processes. Selling pressure from BTC miners has decreased, and BTC activity has dropped to its lowest level in a decade. Steve Cohen has closed down the cryptocurrency division of his hedge fund. Cryptocurrencies were not discussed in the recent Presidential debate. A co-founder of Kraken has donated $1 million to Trump's campaign. Bolivia has lifted its ban on BTC. MegaLabs has successfully raised $20 million and aims to achieve a transaction per second rate of 100,000.

news.headline: Japanese yen plummets to 160 against dollar, lowest since December 1986. Bank of Japan may intervene. Yen spent $61.3 billion on intervention in April-May. Fed and Bank of Japan interest rates diverge. Bank of Japan kept key rate at approx. 0%. U.S. Treasury adds Japan to foreign exchange monitoring list.
news.summary: The Japanese yen has dropped to over 160 per dollar, hitting its lowest level since December 1987, representing a 33% decrease in its value over the past three years. The Bank of Japan had previously intervened by selling forex reserves to control the yen's volatility. In June, the central bank maintained its key short-term interest rate at 0% to 0.1% and hinted at possible reduction in its monthly bond purchases. Japan spent $61.3 billion on currency intervention between late April and late May. The U.S. Treasury Department added Japan to a foreign exchange "monitoring list" for meeting two of the three criteria. This situation suggests a strengthening dollar against the yen. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Traders anticipate significant interest rate cuts of 3 percentage points by March, compared to Fed's forecast of just 25 basis points reduction by 2024.
news.summary: Traders in the U.S. rates options market are anticipating that the Federal Reserve will implement substantial interest-rate cuts beyond its own predictions. They project around 3 percentage points worth of cuts by March, compared to the Fed's forecast of only 25 basis points of reductions by the end of 2024 and a total 125-basis-point cut by the end of next year. This considerable difference in expectations underscores the divergence between market sentiment and the Fed's stated plans for monetary policy.

news.headline: Trump-backed DJT reverses lower after Biden's debate performance criticized. Both appeared on CNN stage.
news.summary: Former President Donald Trump and current President Joe Biden participated in a debate aired on CNN last Thursday. news.summary: DBS Group Holdings Ltd (OTC: DBSDY) has been upgraded to a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) due to an upward trend in earnings estimates. The Zacks rating is determined solely by a company's changing earnings picture. The consensus EPS estimates for the current and following years are tracked by the system. This upgrade reflects positivity about DBS Group's earnings outlook, which could lead to buying pressure and a rise in its stock price. The Zacks rating system considers changing earnings as a strong indicator of near-term stock price movements, which can be more reliable compared to subjective factors that influence Wall Street analyst upgrades. This article provides insights into how the Zacks rating system can be a valuable tool for individual investors in making informed decisions about stock investments.

Daly highlighted the complexity of the current monetary policy landscape, acknowledging the effectiveness of monetary policy in various sectors. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

During the conversation, they delved into Vivendi's strategies regarding a worldwide digital copyright approach and shed light on the impact of token-gated media on the demise of third-party cookies. This initiative is poised to revolutionize digital media consumption, marking a significant shift in the industry's landscape.

news.summary: 21Shares is planning to introduce its spot Solana Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF) in the United States, in response to VanEck's recent filing for the same on Thursday.

news.headline: European regulators rejected PTC Therapeutics' Duchenne muscular dystrophy drug again, causing PTC stock to tumble.
news.summary: European regulators have once again rejected PTC Therapeutics' drug for Duchenne muscular dystrophy, causing a significant drop in the company's stock value early Friday. This repeated rejection adds to PTC's ongoing challenges, with shares tumbling as a result of regulatory decisions.

news.headline: Economists endorse Fed rate cut as inflation drops to 2.6% in May 2024, signaling room for 52 bps cut by Dec. Probable 1st cut in Sept. Fed criticized for late adjustments.
news.summary: Economists have positively received the latest inflation data showing the Personal Consumption Expenditure (PCE) price index dropping to a 2.6% year-on-year rate in May 2024, the lowest since March 2021. This signals progress towards the Federal Reserve's 2% target, potentially leading to interest rate cuts by year-end. The futures market predicts a 52 basis point reduction in the fed funds rate by December, with a 68% chance of the first cut as early as September. Critics like James Thorne of Wellington-Altus believe the Fed is late in adjusting rates, with stagnant price pressures indicating a policy mistake.

news.headline: Chart Industries to provide IPSMR technology to Argent LNG's 20 MTPA facility in Port Fourchon, LA for energy exports.
news.summary: Chart Industries, Inc. (NYSE: GTLS) has recently been awarded a contract by Argent LNG to supply their IPSMR technology for a 20 MTPA LNG facility in Port Fourchon, LA. Argent, based in Louisiana, specializes in providing LNG services and is focused on innovation, efficiency, and environmental sustainability to contribute to the future of U.S. energy exports. Chart Industries' IPSMR solution is known for improving efficiency and performance in liquefaction processes, allowing LNG facilities to maximize resource utilization and lower operational expenses. This modular technology is set to enhance operations at the Port Fourchon facility, optimizing production capabilities.

news.headline: Walgreens Boots Alliance to close 25% stores after reporting $36.35B sales, lowering EPS guidance to $2.80-$2.95. Shares drop 25%.
news.summary: Walgreens Boots Alliance Inc announced third-quarter FY 2024 sales of $36.35 billion, a 2.6% increase YoY (2.5% on constant currency), surpassing the anticipated $36 billion. However, the company adjusted its FY 2024 EPS guidance to $2.80-$2.95 from the previous $3.20-$3.35, falling short of the projected $3.22. Due to this, the pharmacy chain plans to close a substantial number of stores due to financial challenges. The stock plummeted by up to 25% following the earnings report. Barclays downgraded the stock to Underweight, reducing the price target to $11 from $15, citing the EPS reduction.

news.headline: Consensys faces SEC lawsuit for MetaMask Ethereum staking service. Ethereum software giant under enforcement action.
news.summary: Consensys, a leading Ethereum software company, is currently under investigation by US regulators regarding its popular MetaMask wallet. The company is facing potential enforcement action due to concerns raised by the regulator.

news.headline: Mulvihill Capital Management Inc. will consolidate Class A shares at a ratio of 4:1 and exchange preferred shares at a ratio of 1:1.
news.summary: Mulvihill Capital Management Inc. has revealed details regarding the ratios for the Class A share consolidation and the Preferred share exchange. Shareholders will see a consolidation ratio of 1 new share for every 5 existing Class A shares. In addition, there will be a 1-to-1 exchange ratio for Preferred shares. This decision marks a strategic move by Mulvihill Capital Management Inc. to optimize their share structure and enhance shareholder value.

news.headline: AllianceBernstein Natl Municipal Income Fund Q2 earnings show $350,576,798 total net assets, $12.20 NAV per share, as of April 30, 2024.
news.summary: On June 28, 2024, AllianceBernstein National Municipal Income Fund, Inc. (NYSE:AFB) reported its earnings for the Fund's second fiscal quarter concluding on April 30, 2024. The total net assets of the Fund on April 30, 2024, stood at $350,576,798, a decrease from $364,468,619 as of January 31, 2024, and $356,460,126 on April 30, 2023. The net asset value per share of common stock was $12.20 on April 30, 2024, based on 28,744,936 outstanding shares. The detailed financial information is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Trump's debate win lifted S&P 500. Biden's struggle saw futures rise. Investors favor Trump policies, uncertain about future.
news.summary: Following a challenging debate for President Biden, S&P 500 futures saw a slight increase. While some investors may lean towards former President Trump, his policies are not guaranteed to be successful. The market experienced a boost following Trump's perceived debate victory, however, subsequent events led to a different outcome.

news.headline: Inflation hits 3-year low as rate cuts expected, Fed sees 2.6% rate in May. Trump's odds rise to 60% after debate.
news.summary: In May, the Personal Consumption Expenditure price index, the Federal Reserve's key inflation gauge, hit its lowest level since March 2021, recording an annual rate of 2.6%, meeting economists' expectations. This suggests that inflation is aligning with the Fed's 2% target, increasing anticipation for interest rate decreases. Market forecasts now point to a potential rate cut in September, with another by year-end, and possibly three more in the following year. Following the first presidential debate, Donald Trump's chances of winning surged to approximately 60% due to President Joe Biden's perceived lackluster performance. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Michael Jackson passed away leaving a debt of $500 million, owed $40 million to A.E.G. in 2009, now all debts are cleared.
news.summary: In 2009, Jackson reportedly had outstanding debts totaling approximately $40 million to the tour promoter A.E.G. However, the estate's executors have disclosed in a court filing that all these debts have now been fully paid off and eliminated.

news.headline: Wall Street struggles despite drop in inflation rate, with PCE falling to 2.6%, pointing towards Fed's 2% target. Energy stocks rise amid Trump's debate performance analyzing political and economic outcomes.
news.summary: Midday trading on Wall Street was lackluster as major indices slightly dipped despite positive inflation data. In May 2024, the PCE price index, the Federal Reserve’s inflation measure, hit its lowest level since March 2021, showing a 2.6% yearly rate - meeting economists’ expectations. This suggests price pressures are heading towards the Fed’s 2% target, raising expectations for interest rate cuts. Market reactions are cautious, potentially due to analyzing the impact of the recent presidential debate between President Joe Biden and former President Donald Trump. Energy stocks led the S&P 500 sectors. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
